key_concepts: [url]Make Synty Style Low Poly Character in Blender!^^https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5dTEv6OBOjs[/url]
Learn to make a synty style low poly character in blender

content: Delete everything from the general scene. Get your reference images into position.

Synty character models usually have loop around the chin (Image 1). This is where we will start. Add a Plane to the scene, give it an Edge Loop in the y-axis, remove the left vertices and give it a Mirror Modifier in the x-axis with clipping enabled (Image 2). [k]Alt-N,F[/k] to flip the face normals if needed. Move the Plane to the chin area and scale it down (Image 3). The Edge Loop acts to frame the face. In Side View, scale the plane in the y-axis, move it to the chin area and then give it a rotation (Image 4). Select the right edge of the plane and extrude it to the edge of the jawline (Image 5). Go into Side View and move it back slightly to corner of the mouth and rotate the vertices slightly (Image 6). In Front View, extrude the edge to the cheekbone (Image 7). In Side View, move it back slightly to line up with the cheekbone from the side and rotate the edge (Image 8). In Front View, extrude again to the top of the forehead (Image 9) and then rotating the top edge (Image 10).

image.caption: Image 1 - Synty-style head
Image 2 - Adding a Plane to the scene, bisecting it with an Edge Loop in the x-axis, removing the left vertices and giving it a Mirror Modifier in the x-axis with clipping enabled
Image 3 - Moving the Plane to the chin area and scaling it down
Image 4 - Scaling the plane in the y-axis, moving it to the chin area and giving it a rotation
Image 5 - Extruding right edge of the plane to the edge of the jawline
Image 6 - Moving the extrusion back slightly to corner of the mouth and rotating the vertices slightly
Image 7 - Extruding the edge to the cheekbone
Image 8 - Moving the extrusion back slightly to line up with the cheekbone from the side and rotating
Image 9 - Extruding the edge to the top of the forehead
Image 10 - Rotating slightly

content: Select the top Edge Loop and adjust it slightly to create a tapering effect in the Front View (Image 11) and Side View (Image 12). Back in Front View, select the 3 inner vertices and extrude them to the center (Image 13). Fill in the bottom face (Image 14). In Side View, adjust the vertices (Image 15). Select the vertices on the outside and extrude them towards the center of the head, following the jawline (Image 16). Adjust the contours of the line in the Front View (Image 17). From the side, adjust the top vertices to angle down (Image 18). Extrude the vertices out all the way to the back of the head (Image 19). In Top View, move the vertices along the x-axis until they meet the in the middle (Image 20).

image.caption: Image 11 - Adjusting top Edge Loop slightly to create a tapering effect in the Front View
Image 12 - Adjusting top Edge Loop slightly to create a tapering effect in the Side View
Image 13 - Extruding the 3 inner vertices to the center
Image 14 - Filling in the bottom face
Image 15 - Adjusting the vertices in Side View
Image 16 -  Selecting the vertices on the outside and extruding them towards the center of the head, following the jawline
Image 17 - Adjusting the contours of the line in the Front View
Image 18 - Adjusting the top vertices to angle down
Image 19 - Extruding the vertices out all the way to the back of the head
Image 20 - Moving the vertices along the x-axis until they meet the in the middle

content: From the side, adjust the vertices to follow the reference image and align the contours of the edges (Image 21). Select the two middle faces at the top and extrude them along the z-axis (Image 22). From the front, move them along the x-axis until they meet in the middle (Image 23). Add a Loop Cut along the y-axis to the top faces (Image 23). Align the top vertices with the curve of the head (Image 24). Fill in the gap in front with faces (Image 25). Fix the contours of the inner Edge Loop (Image 26). From the front, move that Edge Loop out to round out the skull (Image 27). In Top View, [k]Ctrl-R[/k] to add an Edge Loop at the back of the skull in the z-axis, and then [k]G[/k] to move it out to round out the back (Image 28). Select the vertices in the back and [k]F[/k] to fill in the gaps with faces (Image 29).

image.caption: Image 21 - Adjusting the vertices to follow the reference image and align the contours of the edges
Image 22 - Selecting the two middle faces at the top and extrude them along the z-axis
Image 23 - Moving the faces along the x-axis until they meet in the middle
Image 24 - Aligning the top vertices with the curve of the head
Image 25 - Filling in the gap in front with faces
Image 26 - Fixing the contours of the inner Edge Loop
Image 27 - Moving the Edge Loop out to round out the skull
Image 28 - Adding an Edge Loop at the back of the skull in the z-axis, and moving it out to round out the back
Image 29 - Selecting the vertices in the back and filling in the gaps with faces

content: With the Knife Tool, cut around the ear shape (Image 30). Select the ear faces and [k]E[/k] to extrude them along the x-axis (Image 31). Select the front vertices and Edge Slide them back to the head (Image 32). Adjust the contours of the ear to match the reference image from the front (Image 33) and the side (Image 34).

image.caption: Image 30 - Cut around the ear shape with the Knife Tool
Image 31 - Extruding the ear faces along the x-axis
Image 32 - Edge Sliding the front vertices back to the head
Image 33 - Adjusting the contours of the ear to match the reference image from the front
Image 34 - Adjusting the contours of the ear to match the reference image from the side

content: Rotate around to the bottom and turn off X-Ray Mode ([k]Alt-Z[/k]) in order to see better. Select the two vertices by the chin and back of the skull and create an edge between them ([k]F[/k]) (Image 35). [l]RMB,S[/k] to subdivide the edge and then create a face below the jaw (Image 36). Select the edge in the back and subdivide that, and then create two new faces to close out the head (Image 37). Cut an edge into the face by the front of the jaw (Image 38). In Side view, adjust the edge underneath the jaw to match the reference image (Image 39).

image.caption: Image 35 - Creating an edge between the two vertices by the chin and back of the skull
Image 36 - Subdividing the edge and creating a face below the jaw
Image 37 - Subdividing the edge in the back and creating two new faces to close out the head
Image 38 - Cutting an edge into the face by the front of the jaw
Image 39 - Adjusting the edge underneath the jaw to match the reference image

content: Cut an edge in the upper back of the head and move it out to round out the top of the head (Image 40).

image.caption: Image 40 - Cutting an edge in the upper back of the head and moving it out to round out the top of the head
